desc: Deletes files, folders, or registry keys
reason: Can permanently delete files or directories; requires confirmation to avoid accidental data loss

desc: CMD command to delete one or more files
reason: Deletes files without sending them to the Recycle Bin

desc: CMD command to remove a directory
reason: Can recursively delete entire directory trees

desc: Alias for the CMD rd command
reason: Can recursively delete entire directory trees

desc: Stops a running Windows service
reason: Stopping a service may disrupt dependent applications or system functions

desc: Stops and then starts a Windows service
reason: Briefly interrupts the service and any dependent functionality

desc: Starts a stopped or paused Windows service
reason: Starting an unknown or third-party service may have unintended side effects

desc: Changes the startup type or description of a service
reason: Altering startup type (e.g., to Disabled) can prevent the service from running on boot

desc: Terminates one or more running processes
reason: Can forcibly kill any process including system or application processes, causing data loss

desc: CMD command to terminate processes by name or PID
reason: Can kill any running process including critical background services

desc: Deletes the content of a file without deleting the file itself
reason: Irreversibly clears file contents; data cannot be recovered from the file

desc: Writes or replaces content in a file
reason: Overwrites existing file content completely, causing data loss if used on the wrong file

desc: Appends content to a file
reason: Can corrupt files if appended to binary or configuration files unintentionally

desc: Renames a file, folder, or other item
reason: Renaming system or application files can break functionality

desc: Moves a file or directory to a new location
reason: Moving system or application files can break paths and cause application failure

desc: Deletes a scheduled task from the task scheduler
reason: Permanently removes a task; may break automated maintenance or application routines

desc: Disables a scheduled task so it no longer runs automatically
reason: Prevents scheduled maintenance or application tasks from executing

desc: Enables an optional Windows feature
reason: Installing unexpected features may change system behavior or increase attack surface

desc: Removes an installed software package
reason: Uninstalling a package may remove software that other components depend on

desc: Removes a Windows Server role or feature
reason: Can remove server roles that services or applications depend on

desc: Downloads content from a URL (HTTP/HTTPS)
reason: Can be used to download and save executable files or scripts from the internet

desc: Transfers data from or to a server; alias for Invoke-WebRequest in PowerShell
reason: Can download files or send data to external servers

desc: Downloads files from the web; alias for Invoke-WebRequest in PowerShell
reason: Can download files or scripts from external sources

desc: Starts one or more processes on the local computer
reason: Can launch any executable, including scripts or programs with elevated privileges

desc: CMD command to display or change file attributes
reason: Can hide files or mark them as system files, obscuring them from normal views

desc: CMD command to display or modify access control lists on files and folders
reason: Changing file permissions can lock out users or grant unintended access

desc: CMD command to take ownership of a file or folder
reason: Changing ownership of system files can compromise OS integrity

desc: CMD command to encrypt or decrypt files on NTFS volumes
reason: Encrypting files without saving the key results in permanent data loss

desc: CMD robust file copy tool with overwrite and delete capabilities
reason: With certain flags can overwrite or delete destination files during sync operations

desc: CMD extended copy command that can overwrite files silently
reason: Can overwrite existing files at the destination without prompting

desc: Copies a file, directory, or registry entry to another location
reason: Can silently overwrite existing files at the destination

desc: CMD alias for del, permanently deletes files
reason: Deletes files without sending them to the Recycle Bin

desc: CMD command to move files or directories to another location
reason: Can relocate important files, breaking application paths

desc: CMD command to copy files to another location
reason: Can silently overwrite existing files at the destination
